text.source_text: प्रकरणं चेदं परमात्मनः — ‘ कस्मिन्नु भगवो विज्ञाते सर्वमिदं विज्ञातं भवति ’ (मु. उ. १ । १ । ३) इत्येकविज्ञानेन सर्वविज्ञानापेक्षणात् । परमात्मनि हि सर्वात्मके विज्ञाते सर्वमिदं विज्ञातं स्यात् , न केवले प्राणभृति ॥ ६ ॥
text.transliteration_iast: prakaraṇaṃ cedaṃ paramātmanaḥ — ‘ kasminnu bhagavo vijñāte sarvamidaṃ vijñātaṃ bhavati ’ (mu. u. 1 | 1 | 3) ityekavijñānena sarvavijñānāpekṣaṇāt | paramātmani hi sarvātmake vijñāte sarvamidaṃ vijñātaṃ syāt , na kevale prāṇabhṛti || 6 ||

translation.literal_translation: And this whole context concerns the supreme Self: 'Revered sir, by knowing what does all this become known?' There is expectation of all-knowledge through one knowledge. Only when the supreme all-self is known can all this be known, not by knowing merely the life-bearer.
translation.technical_translation: Sutra 1.3.6 argument from prakarana: Muṇḍaka's ekavijnanena sarvavijnana frame is explicitly Brahman-oriented and cannot terminate in a finite individual knower.
translation.interpretive_note: Context rule: one-knowledge/all-knowledge demand selects Brahman.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Tracks each block inside the section-level inferential sequence

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S03 pass with S04-style inferential framing.
A later philological pass may refine precision around kṣetrajña/upādhi and two-birds imagery terms.
